metadata.file_name: 110. Ang et al. (2020). Big Educational Data & Analytics: Survey, Architecture and Challenges.pdf
metadata.title: Big Educational Data & Analytics: Survey, Architecture and Challenges

survey_design.weight_fields_interpretation: This article is a comprehensive literature review/survey of big educational data and learning analytics paradigms, architectures, data sources, and challenges. It does not analyze a student-level ILSA/NAEP/PISA-style micro-dataset with an explicit sampling frame, nor does it report an analytic sample drawn from a defined assessment cycle. Because there is no primary empirical estimation on a survey-designed student dataset, complex survey weights (e.g., W_FSTUWT/TOTWGT) and replicate-weight procedures (e.g., BRR/jackknife) are not applicable. The only empirical-like material described is an illustrative cross-institution web-scraping scenario, which is not presented as a weighted, population-representative survey analysis.

data.handling_not_reported_explanation: This is a survey/review paper without an original student-level cognitive achievement outcome that would require plausible values (PVs) handling. The paper discusses learning analytics and big-data architectures conceptually and reviews prior studies; it does not report a PV-based dependent variable (e.g., PISA/TIMSS achievement scales) nor a missing-data strategy for PV estimation. Therefore, PV and missing-data handling are not applicable to the review’s own analysis, and missing-data handling is not reported because no original PV-based estimation is conducted.

sample_details.sample_filtering_criteria: No ILSA-style student analytic sample is constructed. The paper is a literature review; it also includes an illustrative cross-institution data collection example in Australia based on web scraping from three universities, but it is not described as a weighted, population-representative student assessment sample.

data.outcome_summary: This paper provides a comprehensive survey of big educational data and analytics, covering data sources (e.g., LMS, MOOC, OER, social media, linked data), data collection/mining/database topics, big-data platforms/architectures (e.g., Hadoop, Spark, Samza), and analytics approaches (predictive analytics, learning analytics, recommendation systems, graph analytics, visual/immersive analytics). It does not conduct an original ML prediction study on an ILSA-style student dataset with a defined outcome, training/validation protocol, or reported performance metrics for a single unified model. Instead, it synthesizes findings from prior works and highlights technological and social challenges (privacy/ethics, interoperability, scalability). As a result, there are no extractable PV-handling, missing-data-handling, or model-performance results for the review itself.
data.null_fields_interpretation: The record is a systematic-style literature review/survey (IEEE Access) rather than an empirical large-scale assessment analysis. Consequently, there is no original student-level dataset with a defined analytic sample size, no PV-based cognitive outcome, no missing-data strategy for an estimation model, and no single ML model trained/evaluated by the authors. The ML-related content is discussed at the level of reviewed approaches and illustrative scenarios, so fields like total_students, countries with per-country Ns, and main_findings for a predictive pipeline are not applicable.
